div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina (Armour) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Buenos Aires, November 3, 1942—9 p.m.
[Received November 3—8:50 p.m.]

div.p:
2169. Our 2149, November 2, 1 p.m.Not printed. and 2157, November 2, 10 p.m. Confirming Ford’sRichard Ford, First Secretary of Embassy. telephone call to Berle this evening, Embassy is sending aide-mémoire with accompanying memorandumSee drafts of memoranda on German Military Espionage in Argentina, p. 218. to Foreign Office at 9 p.m. tonight. As suggested no publicity will be given this material locally pending flash from Department. If and when publicity is given it is suggested it might be preferable for stories to be
written in Washington and cabled here under Washington dateline, we to supply full text of aide-mémoire and memorandum to local offices of Associated Press, United Press, and Reuters.
